title: Question about a version of 20 found On Demand
display_title: Question about a version of 20 found On Demand
wikitext:
[]: Question about a version of 2.0 found On Demand             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Sat Feb 25, 2012 5:48 pm Okay, so back in August, I was browsing through the On Demand stuff on the living room TV and I go to Movie Collections, there one of the collections was "Japanese Anime", so I click it and I find Evangelion 2.0. So obviously, I order it and watch it. Later on, I find that Funimation has their own On Demand section with free and premium anime and they also have the same, but there were some differences. 1. The version under Japanese Anime was 2.0, whereas the version under Funimation Platinum was 2.22 2. 2.0 was subtitled in Japanese and 2.22 was the English dub 3. 2.0 had a TV rating of TV-MA while 2.22 had a TV rating of TV-14 4. 2.0's credits were in Japanese So, does anybody know what was up with this? Why would (I presume it was them who put it up there) Funimation put up 2.0 in Japanese instead of 2.22 in English (or even Japanese, if they could put 2.0 in Japanese, they probably could've done 2.22)

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Feb 25, 2012 7:17 pm Sounds like Funimation made a release of 2.0 to On Demand as soon as they had the rights, and then a polished release later on. They just didn't remove the old one.

Article: Question about a version of 20 found On Demand
[]: Question about a version of 2.0 found On Demand             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Sat Feb 25, 2012 5:48 pm Okay, so back in August, I was browsing through the On Demand stuff on the living room TV and I go to Movie Collections, there one of the collections was "Japanese Anime", so I click it and I find Evangelion 2.0. So obviously, I order it and watch it. Later on, I find that Funimation has their own On Demand section with free and premium anime and they also have the same, but there were some differences. 1. The version under Japanese Anime was 2.0, whereas the version under Funimation Platinum was 2.22 2. 2.0 was subtitled in Japanese and 2.22 was the English dub 3. 2.0 had a TV rating of TV-MA while 2.22 had a TV rating of TV-14 4. 2.0's credits were in Japanese So, does anybody know what was up with this? Why would (I presume it was them who put it up there) Funimation put up 2.0 in Japanese instead of 2.22 in English (or even Japanese, if they could put 2.0 in Japanese, they probably could've done 2.22)

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Feb 25, 2012 7:17 pm Sounds like Funimation made a release of 2.0 to On Demand as soon as they had the rights, and then a polished release later on. They just didn't remove the old one.
